titlestmt.titleproper: Photostats of scattered Indian documents in the Archives of the Indies.9254
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-13 00:13:32 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Photostats of scattered Indian documents in the Archives of the Indies.
did.abstract: Photostats of a selection of important manuscripts and documents regarding Indians from the Archivo General de Indias, Seville, Spain, particularly the section of Papeles procedentes de Cuba.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: List compiled March 20, 1926 by Reina Mercedes. Photostats of a selection of important manuscripts and documents regarding Indians from the Archivo General de Indias, Seville, Spain, particularly the section of Papeles procedentes de Cuba. This includes correspondence, reports to Congress, census information, and treaties, with a focus on New Orleans and southern Indians during the late 1700s. Correspondents include Pierre-Joseph Favrot, John Doughty, Esteban Miró́ and other governors and authority figures. Documents are not necessarily complete, though some are. Probably compiled for someone's research in the 1920s. Paraphrased from the 1926 guide: "Report on a few scattered documents on Indian affaris, found in the General Archvies of the Indies. Attention was confined to communications to and from Indians ("direct discourse"), to treaties, commissions, etc. The documents photocopied are not related to each other, the collection being, in fact, only an indication of what material exists at Seville. The idea was to hear through these documents what the red man had to say for himself, and what was said to him, with as little interference by white mediators as possible."

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Photostats of scattered Indian documents in the Archives of the Indies. #9254. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Cherokee Indians.
Chickasaw Indians.
Comanche Indians.
Creek Indians.
Explorers -- France.
Explorers -- Spain.
Indians of North America
Indians of North America -- Government relations.
Indians of North America -- Treaties.

Pawnee Indians.
Seminole Indians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commission or certificate of captaincy, to be issued to Indians; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, English, Talk of the Baron Carondelet....to great Chief Ifa-acho (4 photosheets)

did.unittitle: # 2 Spanish letter of authority to Indians

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, draft or copy. (2 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/2 pliego, English (1 photosheet)

did.unittitle: This legajo once contained an original of a treaty with Creek Indians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, original, signed by Louis Judice, of aborigines "dependants de la Coste de Louis Judice."; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pliegos of extra size, English. Authenticated copy of a treaty, English-Creek. (5 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego. Authenticated copy, treaty, Spanish-Panis Piques (Comanches). (4 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: British commission English, to Illasatabie, Gorget captain of the Chickesawbays. (1 photosheet)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Census report of the Indian tribes north of Texas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, copy. Maxent's instructions to Fourneret, interpreter, French. Enclosing lists of Upper, Middle and Lower Creeks. Lists are in English.

did.unittitle: Draft, report on or minutes of Congress

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 pliegos, Spanish-Talapuche (Creek), English. In such bad condition it will have to be studied and typed; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pliegos, Spanish-Talapuche (Creek), original, Spanish. (21 pages) (This treaty signed by Alexander McGillvray representing the Creeks.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Tariff to govern Spanish-Creek trade, original. There is an authenticated copy in this same pegajo. (5 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Points to be observed in Indian-Creek relations, in consequence of the Congress. Authenticated copy. There is another similar which indicates that this policy applied also to the Alabamans, Chickasaw and Chactas. (3 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Tariff, Spanish-Chickasaw-Chacta-Alabaman, authenticated copy. (4 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pliego, Treaty, Spanish-Chickasaw, original. (12 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Treaty, Spanish Alibamon. Authenticated copy. (3 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pliegos, Treaty, Spanish-Chacta, authenticated copy. There is indication that the original treaty also was here at one time. There is in this same legajo another authenticated copy. (18 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Miro's instructions to McGillvray (acting as Spanish agent in the Creek Country.) (4 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Treaty, Choctaw-American, original. In P.C. 2360 there is a copy of this treaty and of certain related harangues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliegos, Favrot to Miro, original, enclosing reports on conferences with Chickasaws and Chactas, 2 pliegos, French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Miro to Sonora, original, enclosing 3. Transmitting copy of minutes of conferences held with Chactas and Chicasaws in presence of commissioners from Georgia. Both enclosures dated Yazoo, March 19; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: two - 1 large pliego each, originals. Reports on conference with the Osages. (6 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Miro to Cabello, original (with enclosures) American Indian affairs. Some of these are treaties made by the United States with tribes living south of the Ohio River. The ministers were appointed by George Washington.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Miro to Valiere, original, enclosing 1 sheet, original commission to Pakama, a Miamy Indian, to be Grand medal chief. (3 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Doughty to the Choctaws, English copy, enclosing a message from Washington in New York December 17, 1789, English, copy. (5 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Chickasaw chief, to the commandant at Mibile, French, original. (4 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, The Governor (?) to Tante etoka and others of the Chickasaw Nation, draft, English. (7 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also, P.C. 200; 2 pliegos, Copies, Spanish, of the "Treaty of New York," American-Creek. Other copies in P.C. 1440 and 2362 MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Miro to Las Casas, original, with its enclosures 4, 5, 6 (see note as to Nos. 1-3 on page 1 of enclosure no. 4)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Miro to Las Casas, original, with its enclosures, in part. (20 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Miro to Las Casas, original, with its enclosures, in part. (11 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Miro to Las Casas, original, with enclosures. (11 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, The Governor (?) to "My Children," draft. (3 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, The Governor (?) to Villebeuve, draft, enclosing draft, 1 pliego, proposed treaty with Chactas and Chickasaws and 1 pliego, list of merchandise.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pliegos, Treaty, Spanish Chickasaw-Chacta, copy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Treaty, between Carondelet and McGillvray, English copy. McGillvray acted for the Creek Nation; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Boukfonka. Villebeuve signs and harangue, French, addressed to the Chickasaws, enclosing two related pliegos, all in a cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, The Governor (?) to the chiefs, headmen and warriors of the Cherokee nation, draft, English. (3 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego. The Governor to Pio Mingo and Ugula Yakabe, great chiefs of the Chickasaw Nation, draft, English. (2 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Quesada to Las Casas, original, with enclosures (American-Creek relations) (17 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Benjamin Fooy to Gayoso de Lemos, with enclosures (Pickering to various Chickasaws, Ugula Yackabee to Carondelet, the President to the Chickasaw Nation, Gayoso de Lemos to Ugataphe).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Benjamin Fooy to Gayoso de Lemos, original, with enclosures. (6 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Benjamin Hawkins to Casa Calvo, original, English, enclosing 2 pliegos, English, certain Creeks to Casa Calvo. (8 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Jacob DuBreuill to Vicente Folch, original. Enclosing a talk to the Upper Creeks and their reply. (8 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 small pliego, Jacob DuBreuill to Manuel Farcia, copy. Enclosing tabulation, Seminoles who attended the Congress and original of Spanish-Seminole Treaty August 20, 1802 (9 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 small pliegos, The Governor (?) to Jacob DuBreuill, draft, approving treaty with Seminoles. In P.C. 104 there is an authenticated copy of the treaty (see # 40); and thre is another tabulation dated August 19, of the Seminoles who attended the Congress. There is still another copy of the treaty in P.C. 2337 (4 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, Salcedo (?) to Someruelos, draft, with enclosures: Spanish-American-Creek affairs. (25 photosheets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A talk delivered to the Indians along the Wabash and vicinity by the commandant of Ft. Knox. The Captain advises the Indians to not listen to the words of either French or Spanish emissaries, but to capture such men and bring them to the nearest U.S. post where the captors will be rewarded with gun, ammunition, fifty dollars and a medal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, draft, English translation: Talk of the Baron Carondelet Governor of Louisiana to Great Chief IFA-ACHO of the Creek Nation (4 photostatic sheets); MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 pliego, authenticated copy. Treaty, Spanish-Panis Piques (Comanches) (4 photostatic sheets) Note: This treaty was evidently made between the Spanish and the Comanches with the Pawnees acting as peace commissioners, AW; MISSING

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Points to be Observed in the Treating with the Talapuches, Alibamones, Chicasaws and Chactas." (3 photostatic sheets) Note: This document was listed as being the points to be observed in Indian-Creek affairs, in consequence of the Congress and was dated Pensacola June 1, 1784. It was evidently a case of transposition; MISSING
